text: Hey Naim, I meant I have a list of companies, a list of very relevant companies. So for example, this is a list of companies from a trade show that I attended and all of them are for CPG brands. So I have about, let's say 12, 1300 from there companies list. And then I have also scraped. It was done a long time ago actually. I scraped all the profiles, all the sellers on Amazon and then I filtered them based on their category, those who are in the CPG space. And I also have filtered them based on their company locations. So for example, I mean for now I'm just focusing on the US and Canada ones. I could also do UK, but anyway, so let's say I have about 8,000 companies listed from there that are generating over $5,000 a month in revenue on Amazon in my category. Now I have used Phantom Buster to scrape the LinkedIn URLs. Company URLs. So you see here, I have the LinkedIn company URLs. Now the question I'm asking is if I go to Sales Navigator, I don't have the option to, let's say, okay, let me go to my saved filter first here. So I have these saved filters here. I could specify the company names, but that's, I mean if I have, let's say least a thousand, then I cannot, definitely cannot add them one by one. If there was an option to upload them, that would work. And based on that, I mean it would be ideal if I could do that and then based on, I could apply all the filters that I have applied right now, that would be perfect. So I was wondering if there is a way of doing it in this way or if there is any other way where I can specify the companies, then apply all these filters, then connect with them on LinkedIn. What I'm trying to do is instead of doing it manually, which, I mean it takes time, right? Even let's say going into every company's profile and then selecting the relevant profiles and then sending connection, it takes time, let's say an hour a day, 15 minutes, an hour a day to send 30 requests. So I was just trying to automate the process. So once I have that, let's say I could do it from Sales Navigator, I could upload the company names here and apply all those filters. Then I could use this URL with a software like Linked Helper or anything, Dripify or anything and automate that process. So that's my question. I hope this helps you understand what I mean. Thank you.

chapters.summary: Naim: I have list of companies, a list of very relevant companies. I have used Phantom Buster to scrape the LinkedIn URLs. Now I could specify the companies, then apply all these filters, then connect with them on LinkedIn. I was wondering if there is a way of doing it in this way.
chapters.headline: Naim: I have a list of companies from a trade show
chapters.gist: How to connect with companies on LinkedIn?
